turns.question: How is Axis bank performing in this market
turns.answer:
> **Bottom Line:** Axis Bank is simultaneously winning in private wealth (Burgundy Private AUM +33% YoY to ₹2.07 trillion, 42 cities) and losing in cards (three devaluation rounds, MITC violations, partner purges) — creating a split story that opens a concrete Scapia acquisition window within 30 days.
> As of 30/06/26 · High confidence

SIGNALS: Axis Bank Cards | Axis Burgundy Private | Axis Atlas | Credit Card Devaluation Wave | Premium Travel Card Market

---

## Signal · [[subjects/axis-bank]] · Lens: Fintech
**Stat:** US$27bn Burgundy AUM (+15% YoY) vs Scapia's zero wealth layer · 3 devaluation rounds since 2023 vs Scapia's 1 · MITC-violating overnight cuts vs Scapia's advance notice
- Axis has devalued its card portfolio in April 2025 (Vistara wind-down), April 2026 (partner purge + ratio reversal), and a quiet forfeiture-rule tightening in Oct 2025 — a pattern now reaching mainstream financial media [[subjects/axis-bank]]
- The Burgundy Private AUM milestone ($27bn, 15% YoY) is a genuine moat — 13,000+ families in a full "One Axis" banking relationship Scapia cannot replicate with a standalone credit card [[subjects/axis-bank]]
- Axis is expanding Burgundy Private into 50 cities / 18 states precisely as its card programme burns trust — the wealth arm and the card arm are moving in opposite directions simultaneously [[subjects/axis-burgundy-private]]

---

## Signal · [[subjects/axis-burgundy-private]] · Lens: Fintech
**Stat:** ₹50,000 + GST annual fee vs ₹0 Scapia · 0% forex markup (same as Scapia) · ₹2.07 trillion AUM vs Scapia's no wealth product
- Even at the ₹50,000 fee tier, Burgundy Private Magnus holders received no 30-day MITC notice before the April 2026 ratio was halved from 5:4 to 5:2 — the trust breach is fee-tier agnostic [[subjects/axis-burgundy-private]]
- Burgundy Private's 15 new Tier 2 cities (Raipur, Meerut, Bhubaneswar, Patna, Kozhikode etc.) are branch-and-RM-led; Scapia's mobile-native acquisition can reach the same emerging-affluent travellers faster and at lower cost [Inferred · Medium] [[subjects/axis-burgundy-private]]
- The "One Axis" ecosystem lock-in (banking + loans + insurance + broking + trustee) is a multi-product stickiness moat a travel credit card cannot currently overcome [[subjects/axis-burgundy-private]]

---

## Signal · [[subjects/axis-atlas]] · Lens: Fintech | Travel
**Stat:** ₹5,000 + GST annual fee vs ₹0 Scapia · 3.5% forex markup vs 0% Scapia · 75% transfer value destruction post April 2026 vs stable Scapia coin value
- The April 2026 partner purge removed Accor, Marriott Bonvoy, and Qatar Airways from Travel Edge overnight — without the 30-day MITC notice Axis's own terms require — stranding point-accumulators with no path to their target redemption [[subjects/axis-atlas]]
- Atlas has now undergone three material devaluations (Sep 2023, Apr 2024, Apr 2026); mainstream 2026 consumer guides explicitly flag its 3.5% forex markup as a negative data point in zero-markup comparisons, widening the perception gap [[subjects/axis-atlas]]
- Residual transfer partners (British Airways, KrisFlyer, Flying Blue) at a 2:1 ratio remain a redemption option Scapia does not yet offer — Atlas retains a points-optimiser audience Scapia has not yet addressed [[subjects/axis-atlas]]

---

## Signal · [[subjects/credit-card-devaluation-wave-2025-26]] · Lens: Fintech
**Stat:** 5 major issuers (SBI, Amex, Axis, HDFC, ICICI) all cutting simultaneously vs Scapia's single Feb 2026 adjustment · Fragmented SMS/email disclosure vs public notice · Co-brand discontinuations creating orphaned cardholders
- Axis's devaluation pattern is now industry-confirmed — BankBazaar CEO describes the market shift as structural ("benefit-led to behaviour-led"), not isolated bank decisions [[subjects/credit-card-devaluation-wave-2025-26]]
- SBI Card Etihad Guest discontinuation, Amex new-application pause, and Axis Vistara wind-down collectively orphan a pool of travel-focused co-brand cardholders with no incumbent replacement — a warm acquisition segment for Scapia [Inferred · High] [[subjects/credit-card-devaluation-wave-2025-26]]

---

## Signal · [[subjects/premium-travel-cards-india-market]] · Lens: Fintech | Travel
**Stat:** Banks reducing forex markup to ~2% (still vs 0% Scapia) · Proprietary portals (SmartBuy, iShop, Kotak Unbox) creating closed loops vs Scapia's open in-app booking · Rising acquisition cost pressure across all incumbents
- India's large banks are validating the premium travel card segment by sacrificing short-term forex fee income for HNI wallet share — confirming the segment's structural value and Scapia's total addressable market [[subjects/premium-travel-cards-india-market]]
- The proprietary portal strategy (accelerated rewards only within bank ecosystems) is structurally similar to Scapia's in-app booking model but gated behind annual fees and conditional earn — Scapia offers the same closed-loop travel benefit at ₹0 cost [[subjects/premium-travel-cards-india-market]]

---

## Scapia Implication
- Launch a 30-day "Axis escape" acquisition push targeting Atlas and Magnus Burgundy Private cardholders on Reddit (r/IndiaInvestments, r/CreditCardsIndia) and Twitter/X — owner: growth, window: 30d, target: 500 leads from Axis defectors; lead with the MITC-violation and 75% transfer-value-loss narrative, not just reward rates [[subjects/axis-atlas]]
- Intercept Axis's Tier 2 expansion digitally in the 15 new Burgundy Private cities (Raipur, Bhubaneswar, Patna, Kozhikode) with targeted performance ads before Axis's RM network reaches critical density — owner: performance marketing, window: 60d [Inferred · Medium] [[subjects/axis-burgundy-private]]
- Ensure Scapia is verified as genuinely-zero (no markup, no hidden conversion fee) and submitted to Cardtrail and equivalent comparison guides — owner: BD/content, window: 30d; IDFC FIRST Select currently holds the "best all-rounder" editorial slot Scapia should occupy [[subjects/zero-forex-card-market-india-2026]]
- Brief the growth team on the three-devaluation-pattern story (Sep 2023 → Apr

webResult.summary:
Here's what matters for Scapia's competitive lens:

---

### Axis Bank — Performance Snapshot (FY25/Q1 FY26)

**Overall Financials — Steady but Pressured**
For FY2025, Axis Bank posted a 6% increase in net profit to ₹26,373 crore, with net interest income up 9% and non-interest income up 13%, pushing net total income to ₹79,605 crore. However, in Q1 FY26, Net Interest Income was up only 1% YoY and down 2% QoQ, with NIM compressing to 3.80% — signaling margin headwinds. Asset quality also slipped slightly, with GNPA rising to 1.57% and NNPA at 0.45%, partly attributed to a "technical impact" in Q1 FY26.

**Credit Cards — Relevant Directly to Scapia**
Axis Bank holds a 13.6% share of India's credit card market, with its travel-and-rewards positioning drawing strong demand from frequent travellers. Among top issuers, HDFC led with 12% YoY growth, followed by SBI at 9% and Axis at 5%, holding 13.6% market share. In Q1 FY26 alone, Axis acquired ~0.79 million new credit cards, maintaining a ~14% CIF market share.

**Co-Brand & Travel Card Strategy — Direct Competitive Overlap**
Axis typically leads in co-branded activations (Flipkart, Vistara), with flagship travel cards — Magnus, Ace, and Atlas — targeting high-income travellers and travel enthusiasts. This is Scapia's exact target demographic.

**Bottom line for Scapia:** Axis is slowing in card issuance growth (only 5% YoY) and facing NIM pressure, but its travel-card franchise (Atlas, Magnus) remains deeply entrenched at ~14% market share. Sc

turns.question: Tell me about HDFC regalia
turns.answer:
> **Bottom Line:** HDFC Regalia Gold's June 2026 revamp tightens eligibility and caps rewards, creating an immediate 30-day window for Scapia to capture the excluded aspirational and international-traveller segments.
> As of 22/06/26 · High confidence

SIGNALS: HDFC Regalia Gold | Forex & Fees | Lounge & Benefits | Eligibility Gap

---

## Signal · [[subjects/hdfc-regalia-gold]] · Lens: Fintech
**Stat:** ₹2,500 annual fee (vs Scapia ₹0) · 3.5% forex markup (vs Scapia 0%) · 4 pts/₹150 spend with utility/telecom caps (vs Scapia 4% on in-app travel, uncapped)
- HDFC revamped Regalia Gold on 2026-06-22 with a ₹2,500 joining + annual fee waived only on ₹4,00,000 annual spend, targeting the mass-premium salaried segment [[subjects/hdfc-regalia-gold]]
- Utility and telecom reward categories now carry a monthly cap, reducing effective earn rate for high-frequency bill-payers — a deliberate margin-protection move per Parag Rao [[subjects/hdfc-regalia-gold]]
- HDFC's stated posture is "rewards must be sustainable, not a giveaway" — [Inferred · High] this signals further devaluation cycles ahead, not a one-time fix [[subjects/hdfc-regalia-gold]]

---

## Signal · [[subjects/hdfc-regalia-gold]] · Lens: Fintech
**Stat:** 3.5% forex markup (vs Scapia 0%) · ₹2,500 fee barrier (vs Scapia ₹0) · fee waiver requires ₹4L spend (vs Scapia no spend requirement)
- Forex markup remains unchanged at 3.5%, making Regalia Gold structurally uncompetitive for international travellers versus zero-markup alternatives [[subjects/hdfc-regalia-gold]]
- The ₹4,00,000 spend-waiver threshold means most moderate spenders pay ₹2,500/year in perpetuity — Scapia's lifetime-free positioning directly undercuts this [[subjects/hdfc-regalia-gold]]

---

## Signal · [[subjects/hdfc-regalia-gold]] · Lens: Travel
**Stat:** 12 domestic lounge visits/year (vs Scapia unlimited on ₹20K milestone) · no international lounge (vs Scapia Airport Privileges up to ₹2,000/trip) · 2,500 welcome points on activation
- Regalia Gold's hard cap of 12 domestic lounge visits per year is a concrete, quantifiable disadvantage against Scapia's unlimited domestic lounge benefit [[subjects/hdfc-regalia-gold]]
- Airport Privileges (coin-back on dining/shopping/spa) give Scapia an international-airport story that Regalia Gold entirely lacks [[subjects/hdfc-regalia-gold]]

---

## Signal · [[subjects/hdfc-regalia-gold]] · Lens: Fintech
**Stat:** ₹1,00,000/month salaried income required (vs Scapia ~₹35,000/month) · age 21–60 (vs Scapia ~21–65) · self-employed ITR >₹12L required
- Regalia Gold's tightened eligibility — ₹1,00,000/month salaried or ITR >₹12L — excludes a large aspirational-traveller cohort that sits squarely in Scapia's addressable base [[subjects/hdfc-regalia-gold]]
- [Inferred · High] Every Regalia Gold rejection at the income threshold is a warm lead for Scapia, particularly in tier-2/3 cities where Scapia is actively expanding [[subjects/hdfc-regalia-gold]]

---

## Scapia Implication
- **Run a targeted forex-savings campaign immediately** — owner: growth/marketing, window: 30d, target: quantify and publish the "save 3.5% on every international spend" message in paid and creator channels before HDFC's revamp news cycle fades.
- **Launch a comparison acquisition push on lounge** — owner: growth, window: 30d, target: travel-creator and comparison-site content contrasting unlimited Scapia lounge vs Regalia Gold's 12-visit cap; aim for 3–5 high-reach placements.
- **Activate a ₹1L income-gap acquisition funnel** — owner: growth + credit, window: 45d, target: surface Scapia to applicants rejected or priced out by Regalia Gold's salaried eligibility bar, especially in tier-2/3 cities.
- **Monitor Regalia Gold community sentiment** — owner: product marketing, window: ongoing, target: watch Reddit/Twitter for switcher intent triggered by utility/telecom reward caps and move with targeted win-back offers within 48h of spikes.

---

**Confidence:** High — based on a dated, detailed product revamp filing in [[subjects/hdfc-regalia-gold]] with direct spec comparisons and a named leadership quote from 2026-06-22.

turns.question: Go deeper: HDFC Regalia Gold
turns.answer:
**HDFC Regalia Gold is mid-devaluation cycle — simultaneous cuts to earn rate, lounge conditionality, and forex cost create a compounding value destruction that peaks on July 1, exactly when Scapia's unconditional proposition is strongest.**

**Stat:** 6% reward rate cut (Scapia earns 4% in-app, 2% elsewhere, free) · Up to 5.25% total forex cost on INR international transactions (Scapia 0%) · ₹2,500 annual fee + ₹4L spend-waiver pressure (Scapia ₹0 lifetime) [[subjects/hdfc-regalia-gold]]

### Where Scapia wins
- Lounge access is now gated behind ₹60,000/quarter from July 1 — cardholders who miss that threshold lose all 3 visits; Scapia's lounge unlocks at ₹20,000/month with no quarterly cliff [[subjects/hdfc-regalia-gold]]
- The DCC fee addition (1.75%, effective May 15) stacks onto the existing 3.5% forex markup — international travellers paying in INR now absorb up to 5.25% total cost per transaction vs Scapia's hard zero [[subjects/hdfc-regalia-gold]]
- Regalia Gold's effective earn rate post-cut is 1.625% (5 points/₹200) on general spend; Scapia earns 2% on all non-travel Visa spend and 4% on in-app travel — a structural rate advantage at no annual fee [[subjects/hdfc-regalia-gold]]
- Multiple simultaneous changes — reward cut, lounge condition, reissuance fee (₹199), DCC fee — compound cardholder confusion; Scapia's single-layer earn-and-redeem structure is a clarity advantage that acquisition messaging can exploit directly [[subjects/hdfc-regalia-gold]]

### Where they're ahead
- 6 international Priority Pass lounge visits remain intact and unconditional — Scapia's international airport benefit is coin-back on dining/shopping/spa capped at ₹2,000/trip, conditional on an in-app international booking ≥₹50,000, which is a materially weaker offer for heavy international travellers [[subjects/hdfc-regalia-gold]] [[scapia]]
- The new Boarding Edge Programme (spa, buffet, upgrade, airport transfer) is a tangible novelty perk that provides perceived premium differentiation Scapia currently has no direct equivalent for

turns.question: Tell me about wise, how are they doing good in the 0 forex
turns.answer:
> **Bottom Line:** Wise Travel Card dominates the Indian prepaid forex card category in 2026 consumer guides but remains structurally inferior to Scapia's zero-markup credit model — Scapia should publish a direct credit-vs-prepaid comparison within 30 days to capture the research intent Wise currently owns.
> As of 29/06/26 · High confidence

SIGNALS: Wise Travel Card | Zero-Forex Card Market India | Forex Card vs Credit Card

---

## Signal · [[subjects/wise-travel-card]] · Lens: Fintech | Travel
**Stat:** 40+ currencies (most in India vs Scapia's transaction-time 0% markup) · 0% markup at interbank rate (vs Scapia's 0% markup on credit) · USD 200/mo free ATM (vs Scapia: cash advance fees apply)
- Wise holds the #1 "best overall forex card" ranking in every major 2026 Indian consumer guide, including Cardtrail and standalone international travel roundups, citing interbank rate across 40+ currencies and zero annual/inactivity fee [[subjects/wise-travel-card]]
- Three separate 2026 guides (May, June x2) named Wise the default prepaid forex recommendation — including for HNI travellers spending ₹8L–₹20L annually on international travel, meaning Wise is winning the research moment before users reach credit card comparisons [[subjects/wise-travel-card]]
- Wise's core structural weakness is prepaid: customers must lock INR capital upfront, face LRS compliance on loads, and earn zero rewards — none of which apply to Scapia [[subjects/wise-travel-card]]

---

## Signal · [[subjects/zero-forex-card-market-india-2026]] · Lens: Fintech
**Stat:** ₹3,000–7,000 markup cost per ₹2L trip (Scapia saves this entirely) · 18% GST on markup charges (zero on Scapia) · only 3 verified genuinely-zero-markup cards in Cardtrail's ranked tier (Scapia absent)
- Consumer guides are now formalising zero-forex rankings with verified real-cost framing, shifting card selection from brand recognition to transparent price comparison — accelerating category awareness among international travellers [[subjects/zero-forex-card-market-india-2026]]
- Guides flag hidden conversion fees (separate 1% on top of "zero markup"), raising the bar for issuers to prove genuine zero — Scapia's Federal Bank co-brand structure or content gap may explain its absence from the verified-zero tier, which is a compounding SEO loss [Inferred · High] [[subjects/zero-forex-card-market-india-2026]]
- IDFC FIRST Select currently holds the "best all-rounder" editorial slot that Scapia should occupy; absence from top-ranked positions in a growing guide category represents a mounting acquisition cost [[subjects/zero-forex-card-market-india-2026]]

---

## Signal · [[subjects/forex-credit-card-india-travel]] · Lens: Fintech | Travel
**Stat:** USD 31.7B LRS outflow FY25 (+17% YoY) · 56% of LRS = travel (largest category) · 20% TCS above ₹7L applies to forex card loads (not to credit card international spend)
- Standard Indian credit cards cost 3.5–4.1% on overseas swipes (markup + GST); zero-markup credit cards including Scapia now structurally match or beat prepaid forex card economics once issuance, reload, and ATM fees are included [[subjects/forex-credit-card-india-travel]]
- TCS at 20% above ₹7L cumulative LRS applies to forex card loads and travel remittances — credit card international spend does not trigger LRS/TCS in the same way, making Scapia's model cash-flow-superior for heavy travellers [Inferred · High] [[subjects/forex-credit-card-india-travel]]
- DCC (Dynamic Currency Conversion) averages 5.4% excess charge at European POS per 2024 MoneyControl analysis; Scapia's 0% markup removes all of this friction, but this messaging is absent from current Scapia content [[subjects/forex-credit-card-india-travel]]

---

## Scapia Implication
- Publish a "forex card vs zero-markup credit card" comparison landing page within 30 days — owner: content/growth, target: rank in Cardtrail/NerdWallet-style guides for "best zero forex card India 2026" — explicitly surfacing capital lock-in cost, LRS/TCS friction, and zero rewards as the prepaid card's hidden costs [[subjects/wise-travel-card]] [[subjects/zero-forex-card-market-india-2026]]
- Run paid and organic outreach to the 6-8 week pre-travel research window — owner: growth, window: 30d — insert the credit-over-prepaid argument at the moment Wise is being recommended, before users anchor on the forex card category [[subjects/wise-travel-card]]
- Engage Cardtrail and top Indian forex card guides for verified zero-markup listing — owner: partnerships/PR, window: 14d — confirm Scapia qualifies as genuinely zero (no hidden conversion fee) and secure inclusion in the ranked tier currently dominated by IDFC FIRST [[subjects/zero-forex-card-market-india-2026]]
- Build DCC-awareness content (₹10,800 lost on a ₹2L trip example) as a social/CRM hook — owner: content, window: 30d — this is an under-exploited, emotionally salient number that directly favours Scapia's 0% model [[subjects/forex-credit-card-india-travel]]

---

**Confidence:** High — three independent 2026 consumer guides corroborate Wise's category dominance, and the LRS/TCS/DCC data is sourced from verifiable regulatory and MoneyControl analysis

webResult.summary:
Here's what matters for Scapia leaders on Wise's zero-forex model and performance:

---

**How Wise wins on zero forex markup:**

Wise uses the mid-market exchange rate — the rate you see on Google or Reuters — with zero markup applied to the conversion. Most banks don't pass this rate through; instead, they apply a 2–4% hidden markup, presenting the inflated rate as simply "today's exchange rate." Wise's model flips this: it charges the mid-market rate with no markup, and instead levies a clearly stated upfront fee, so users know exactly what they pay and what the recipient receives before confirming. That transparent transfer fee ranges from 0.33–1% depending on the currency corridor.

**The cost gap vs. banks (Scapia's competitive context):**

Sending $1,000 through Wise costs roughly $5–$15 all-in (~0.5–1.5%), and the gap widens as transfer size grows, because the bank's FX markup scales with the amount while Wise's percentage fee stays consistent.

**Business performance — is it working?**

FY2025 saw 16% underlying income growth (18% in constant currency) with a profit before tax margin of 21%, above their medium-term target of 13–16%. In H1 FY26 (ended Sept 2025), active customers hit 13.4M — up 18% YoY — with cross-border volume up 24% to £84.9bn and customer holdings up 37% to £25.3bn.

**Scapia relevance:** Wise's success validates that Indian and global travelers will migrate to zero/low-forex products at scale. The risk for Scapia is that Wise increasingly competes for the same "international spender" wallet — though Wise's debit card isn't available to Indian businesses and its card rewards model differs from a co-branded credit card like Scapia's. Wise's own earnings call flagged that

webResult.summary:
Here's the tight market intelligence summary on HDFC's trends — what matters for Scapia:

---

### 📊 HDFC Credit Card Market Trends — Key Signals for Scapia

**1. Market Dominance, but Tightening Benefits**
HDFC Bank commands ~22.3% of India's credit card market in 2025, leading on both spend and cardholders due to popular reward structures. However, HDFC has rolled out benefit cuts in phases: lounge access rule changes from June 10, 2025; reward & fee revisions from July 1, 2025; a broader fee and access overhaul from January 1, 2026; and an Infinia redemption cap from February 1, 2026.

**2. Lounge Access Economics Deteriorating for Incumbents**
Lounge costs are rising — airports like BLR, DEL, and BOM have seen lounge traffic explode, and banks are paying per visit. The economics don't work when every cardholder walks in quarterly without spending enough to justify it. This is a structural vulnerability Scapia can exploit with its spend-linked, transparent lounge model.

**3. Industry-Wide Rewards Devaluation — Not Just HDFC**
This is an industry-wide trend in India, not just an HDFC move — SBI and ICICI Bank have both tied lounge access to spend thresholds, and ICICI is also revising reward structures on cards like Mayura and Ashva. Scapia's no-annual-fee, travel-first positioning becomes a cleaner differentiation story as incumbents claw back benefits.

**4. Transfer Partner Expansion as a Loyalty Play**
As of July 2025, HDFC Bank has 20+ transfer partners, giving cardholders more ways than ever to turn reward points into free flights or hotel stays. This signals HDFC doubling down on aspirational travel redemptions — a space Scapia competes in directly.

---

**Bottom Line for Scapia:** HDFC's benefit cuts and spend-gating of lounge access are

turns.question: Go deeper: HDFC Regalia Gold
turns.answer:
**HDFC's Regalia Gold revamp is a deliberate margin-protection move that simultaneously tightens the product and concentrates competitive pressure on Scapia's exact core audience — the aspirational, travel-inclined salaried Indian.**

**Stat:** ₹2,500 annual fee (Scapia ₹0) · 3.5% forex markup (Scapia 0%) · 12 lounge visits/year (Scapia unlimited via RuPay milestone)

### Where Scapia wins
- Forex savings are structural, not promotional: at 3.5% markup, a traveller spending ₹1L internationally pays ₹3,500 in hidden fees — Scapia charges zero, a gap that compounds every trip [[scapia#2-product-mechanics]]
- Scapia's lifetime-free model eliminates the ₹4L annual spend-waiver trap entirely; users who spend below that threshold on Regalia Gold pay ₹2,500 + GST for the privilege [[subjects/hdfc-regalia-gold]]
- Unlimited domestic lounge access via the ₹20,000 previous-cycle milestone structurally beats a hard 12-visit annual cap for any frequent flyer — even post the Feb 2026 milestone doubling [[scapia#2-product-mechanics]]
- Regalia Gold's ₹1L/month salaried income floor is a hard eligibility wall; Scapia's ~₹35,000/month threshold opens the card to a far larger slice of the 25–35 urban traveller cohort Scapia targets [[subjects/hdfc-regalia-gold]] [[scapia#1-identity]]

### Where they're ahead
- HDFC's branch and distribution network gives Regalia Gold ambient trust that a 2022-founded fintech cannot manufacture quickly — especially in tier-2/3 cities where Scapia is now expanding [[scapia#4-current-strategic-posture]]
- The 2,500 welcome reward points on activation is a concrete, immediate hook; Scapia's onboarding value is deferred and tied to in-app travel booking behaviour, which requires a behaviour change to realise [[subjects/hdfc-regalia-gold]]
- Regalia Gold's mass-premium salaried positioning is backed by HDFC's existing relationship with payroll-linked salary account holders — an acquisition channel Scapia has no equivalent of `[Inferred · High]`

### Recent moves
- 2026-06-22: HDFC revamped Reg

turns.question: What are the top competitors to Scapia's credit card
turns.answer:
> **Bottom Line:** As of today, Scapia's most material competitors are Axis Atlas (miles-travel card, now closed to new applicants), IDFC FIRST Mayura (premium zero-forex), Ixigo AU (lifetime-free zero-forex travel co-brand), and HDFC Infinia/DCB (super-premium unlimited lounge) — each threatening a distinct Scapia user segment within the next 90 days.
> As of 22/06/26 · High confidence

SIGNALS: Axis Atlas | Ixigo AU | IDFC FIRST Mayura | HDFC Infinia | HDFC Diners Club Black | ICICI Emeralde | RBL World Safari | OneCard | Niyo Global SBM | Uni NxtWave | IDFC FIRST WOW! | Axis Burgundy Private

---

## Signal · [[subjects/axis-atlas]] · Lens: Fintech
**Stat:** ₹5,000+GST annual fee (vs ₹0 Scapia) · 3.5% forex markup (vs 0% Scapia) · tiered 4–12 intl lounge (vs spend-triggered domestic unlimited Scapia)
- Axis Atlas is the closest direct travel-miles rival but discontinued to new applicants in 2026, making every stranded Atlas holder an immediate Scapia acquisition target [[subjects/axis-atlas]].
- Strong transfer partner ecosystem (1:2 to 20+ airlines/hotels) is Atlas's residual moat — Scapia has no transferable miles, only in-app coin redemption [[subjects/axis-atlas]].
- 3.5% forex markup is Atlas's biggest weakness; Scapia's 0% forex wins decisively on any international trip [[subjects/axis-atlas]].

---

## Signal · [[subjects/ixigo-au]] · Lens: Fintech
**Stat:** ₹0 annual fee (vs ₹0 Scapia) · 0% forex markup (vs 0% Scapia) · 16 domestic + 1 intl lounge/year (vs spend-triggered domestic Scapia)
- Ixigo AU is the most structurally similar competitor — lifetime-free, zero-forex, travel co-brand targeting the exact same digitally-native Indian traveller [[subjects/ixigo-au]].
- Ixigo AU's 10% discount on ixigo bookings is a direct threat to Scapia's in-app booking moat, particularly for trains and buses where ixigo is dominant [[subjects/ixigo-au]].
- Scapia's 4% in-app travel earn rate (20 coins/₹100) likely outperforms Ixigo AU's 10 pts/₹200 online earn on flights and hotels [Inferred · Medium] — but this needs explicit side-by-side positioning in Scapia's marketing.

---

## Signal · [[subjects/idfc-first-mayura]] · Lens: Fintech
**Stat:** ₹5,999+GST annual fee (vs ₹0 Scapia) · 0% forex markup (vs 0% Scapia) · 16 domestic + 16 intl lounge/year (vs spend-triggered domestic Scapia)
- IDFC FIRST Mayura is the strongest zero-forex premium threat, matching Scapia on forex while offering vastly superior lounge access (16 intl visits vs Scapia's zero international lounge) [[subjects/idfc-first-mayura]].
- Up to 60X rewards on partner spends challenges Scapia's 4% in-app travel earn as the headline value proposition for high-spenders [[subjects/idfc-first-mayura]].
- Scapia's ₹0 fee vs Mayura's ₹5,999 is the decisive counterpoint for budget-conscious travellers who don't hit high enough spend to justify the fee [[subjects/idfc-first-mayura]].

---

## Signal · [[subjects/hdfc-infinia]] · Lens: Fintech
**Stat:** ₹12,500+GST annual fee (vs ₹0 Scapia) · 2% forex markup (vs 0% Scapia) · unlimited domestic + intl Priority Pass lounge (vs spend-triggered domestic Scapia)
- HDFC Infinia is the invite-only super-premium ceiling competing for Scapia's aspiring HNI segment, but its ₹12,500 fee and invite-only access mean it targets a non-overlapping income tier [Inferred · High] [[subjects/hdfc-infinia]].
- Unlimited international lounge via Priority Pass is Infinia's clearest benefit gap over Scapia, which offers zero international lounge visits [[subjects/hdfc-infinia]].
- Scapia's 0% forex beats Infinia's 2% markup on every international transaction, a recurring and compounding savings for frequent international travellers [[subjects/hdfc-infinia]].

---

## Signal · [[subjects/hdfc-diners-black]] · Lens: Fintech
**Stat:** ₹10,000+GST annual fee (vs ₹0 Scapia) · 2% forex markup (vs 0% Scapia) · unlimited domestic + intl Diners Club lounge (vs spend-triggered domestic Scapia)
- HDFC DCB Metal's unlimited lounge at 1,300+ Diners Club locations worldwide is a direct challenge to Scapia users who want international lounge access [[subjects/hdfc-diners-black]].
- 10X SmartBuy rewards drive outsized value for HDFC ecosystem spenders, a cohort Scapia must convert with its in-app travel earn rate rather than a points ecosystem [[subjects/hdfc-diners-black]].

---

## Signal · [[subjects/icici-emeralde]] · Lens: Fintech
**Stat:** ₹12,000+GST annual fee (vs ₹0 Scapia) · 2% forex markup (vs 0% Scapia) · unlimited domestic + intl Priority Pass lounge (vs spend-triggered domestic Scapia)
- ICICI Emeralde competes on unlimited lounge and brand prestige but its effective reward redemption value (₹0.25/pt) is far weaker than Scapia's 4% in-app travel earn [[subjects/icici-emeralde]].
- Scapia wins cleanly on forex (0% vs 2%) and fee (₹0 vs ₹12,000) — Emeralde is an upgrade risk only for users who prioritise unlimited lounge over cost efficiency [[subjects/icici-emeralde]].

---

## Signal · [[subjects/rbl-world-safari]] · Lens: Fintech
**Stat:** ₹3,000+GST annual fee (vs ₹0 Scapia) · 0% forex markup (vs 0% Scapia) · 2 intl + 8 domestic lounge/year (vs spend-triggered domestic Scapia)
- RBL World Safari competes on zero forex and travel rewards at a mid-tier fee, but awards no reward points on international transactions — a significant structural weakness [[subjects/rbl-world-safari]].
- Scapia's lifetime-free positioning beats the ₹3,000 fee decisively for cost-sensitive travellers [[subjects/rbl-world-safari]].

related.headline: Ixigo AU is a lifetime-free zero-forex travel co-brand competing directly with Scapia on the same user
IDFC FIRST Mayura is the strongest zero-forex premium card with 16 intl lounge visits and up to 60X rewards
HDFC Infinia is the invite-only super-premium ceiling with 2% forex and unlimited lounge
Axis Atlas is the miles-centric travel card with 3.5% forex and tiered lounge but strong transfer partners
HDFC DCB Metal delivers unlimited lounge, 10X SmartBuy rewards and 2% forex at ₹10K fee
